name: Plaza Bakery's Cream Cheese Topped Chocolate
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Chocolate, bittersweet or semi-sweet, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Lightly grease a 9-inch square baking pan. The pan must be at least 2 inches deep.
3. Dust the pan with flour and tap out excess.
4. Using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese with sugar in a medium bowl until smooth. Blend in egg, vanilla, and salt. Set topping aside while preparing brownie batter.
5. For Brownies:
- Melt chocolate and butter in a heavy saucepan over very low heat, stirring until smooth.
- Cool slightly.
- Using an electric mixer, beat eggs, sugar, vanilla, and salt in a large bowl until thick, about 3 minutes. Stir in melted chocolate, then flour and semi-sweet chocolate chips.
6. Pour batter into the prepared pan; smooth top.
7. Spoon cream cheese topping into a pastry bag fitted with a 1/2-inch round tip. Pipe cheese mixture in diagonal lines atop brownie batter, spacing lines 1/2 to 3/4 inches apart.
8. Run the tip of a small knife through the cream cheese on opposite diagonals, spacing 1/2 inch apart and creating a V pattern.
9. Bake until a toothpick inserted comes out with a few moist crumbs still attached, about 40 minutes.
10. Cool dessert in pan on a rack for 3 hours. Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours. Can be prepared up to 2 days ahead. Run a sharp knife around the pan to loosen. Cut dessert into 12 brownies. Serve cold.
